channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Dad treks miles through Hurricane Helene aftermath to walk daughter down aisle on her wedding day
item.description: A father's love knew no boundaries over the weekend as he walked nearly 30 miles through flood debris in South Carolina to Tennessee to walk his daughter down the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nantucket's migrant crisis handling called out after quiet island rocked by wave of violent attacks
item.description: Toby Brown, head of Nantucket's GOP, wants more vetting on the island after illegal migrants accused of sex crimes and assault there were seized by ICE.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Helene's death toll surpasses 150 as crews continue to search for survivors
item.description: Exhausted emergency workers continued to work around-the-clock to clear roads, restore power and cellphone service, and reach people left stranded by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum will keep doing mañanera briefings, though her style is unlike her predecessor's
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico's new President, Claudia Sheinbaum, will start her day Wednesday much like her political mentor, ex-President Andrés Manuel López Obrador, began most of his: with an early morning press briefing from the National Palace.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: US 'Welcome Corps' helps resettle LGBTQ+ refugees fleeing crackdowns against gay people
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Cabrel Ngounou's life in Cameroon quickly unraveled after neighbors caught the teenager with his boyfriend. A crowd surrounded his boyfriend's house and beat him. Ngounou's family learned of the relationship and kicked him out.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and TERRY CHEA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Vance, Walz address address school shootings
item.description: Walz said the solution is more research into gun violence. Vance said we have to increase security in our schools.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Helene death toll now at least 166 as Biden plans to visit ravaged Carolinas
item.description: President Joe Biden will survey the devastation in North and South Carolina as rescuers continued their search for anyone still unaccounted for since Hurricane Helene caused catastrophic damage across the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: Erik Verduzco, Jeff Amy And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Doctor charged in connection with Matthew Perry’s death is expected to plead guilty
item.description: A San Diego doctor charged as part of the investigation of the overdose death of Matthew Perry is expected to plead guilty in a federal court in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dalton, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New York City Mayor Eric Adams is due back in court in his criminal case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to return to court Wednesday in a case where he is accused of taking bribes and illegal campaign contributions. The Democrat is set to make a 10:30 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KTLA 5

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says he would veto national abortion ban
item.description: Former President Trump on Tuesday said he would veto a federal abortion ban if it made it to his desk in a potential second term after previously refusing to say whether he would do so.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Walz misleadingly claims to have been in Hong Kong during period tied to Tiananmen Square massacre
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Multiple news reports indicate that Democratic vice presidential nominee Minnesota Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and DIDI TANG Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What was the QR code on screen during the vice presidential debate?
item.description: CBS News is getting creative as it attempts to conduct Tuesday's Vice Presidential debate fairly and productively.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge rejects computer repairman's defamation claims over reports on Hunter Biden laptop
item.description: DOVER, Del. (AP) — A computer repairman at the center of a controversy over Hunter Biden's laptop has lost his defamation case against news outlets, the president's son and Joe Biden's presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs accused of sexual misconduct by 120 people, attorney says
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — An attorney said Tuesday he is representing 120 accusers who have come forward with sexual misconduct allegations against Sean “Diddy” Combs, the hip-hop mogul who is awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: Trump leads with 49 percent support, Harris earns 47 percent support, Green Party candidate Jill Stein gets 1 percent support and Justice For All Party candidate Cornel West gets 1 percent support in the Quinnipiac University poll conducted in late September and released Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Trump takes lead over Harris in North Carolina Quinnipiac survey
item.description: The poll offers much better news for Trump than last month’s poll from Quinnipiac, which found Harris leading Trump by 3 points.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: McDonald’s is bringing back its famous ‘Boo Buckets,’ with a new color
item.description: McDonald's famous Boo Buckets are making a return just in time for Halloween, and a new color has joined the lineup this year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dockworkers may have the negotiating advantage in their strike against US ports
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The 45,000 dockworkers who went on strike Tuesday for the first time in decades at 36 U.S. ports from Maine to Texas may wield the upper hand in their standoff with port operators over wages and the use of automation.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and TASSANEE VEJPONGSA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Is the food in the fridge still good? California wants to end the guessing game
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California wants to help end the everyday household debate over whether the food in the fridge is still good to eat. Food labels that say “sell by” or “best before” are misleading because they have no universal meaning under current laws.
item.dc:creator: TRÂN NGUYỄN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: iPhone stuck in SOS mode? Here's how to get out of it
item.description: There are ongoing reports of a widespread outage impacting multiple cellphone and internet providers Monday morning. According to Downdetector.com, AT&T, Verizon and T-Mobile users have all experienced issues.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, NewsNation

item.title: Workday down? User reports indicate problems with platform
item.description: Is your Workday down? You might not be alone. According to Downdetector.com, reports of people having problems with Workday spiked shortly after 10 a.m. Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, Fox 5

item.title: A judge considers whether to clarify 2 new Georgia election certification rules
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A judge is considering whether to issue clarification or guidance for two new rules from the Georgia State Election Board that have to do with county certification of election results.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A whopping 40 trillion gallons of rain fell on the South thanks to Helene and other storms
item.description: That's enough to fill the Dallas Cowboys' stadium 51,000 times, or 60 million Olympic-size swimming pools.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NHC tracking disturbance near where Helene formed | Tracking the Tropics
item.description: How strong it will be and where is it going to go? This afternoon on Tracking the Tropics, we'll break down what we know about the disturbance and where it could be headed.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: New bill proposes panic buttons for Ohio's schools
item.description: Pressing the button three times signifies a medical emergency and pressing it eight or more times means there is an active shooter situation.
item.dc:creator: Delaney Ruth, NewsNation

item.title: My car was submerged in floodwaters. Should I start it?
item.description: Storm surges and flooding from Hurricane Helene have caused widespread devastation across parts of the Southeastern U.S., in some cases leaving homes and cars completely underwater.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Helene's death toll surpasses 150 as crews continue to search for survivors
item.description: Exhausted emergency workers continued to work around-the-clock to clear roads, restore power and cellphone service, and reach people left stranded by Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: These are the 50 best school districts in America for 2025, report finds
item.description: Choosing where to live could come down to the strength of the school district for many parents, and a new study ranks the top 50 in the nation.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Submerged EVs can catch fire when inundated with saltwater: What to know
item.description: Electric vehicles can catch fire if they are inundated by saltwater, so owners who live in the path of a major storm should take precautions and prepare for the possibility that they'll be unable to charge their cars during a power outage.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, KRON4

item.title: Donate today to help Red Cross efforts across 10 states
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Days after Hurricane Helene's deadly force tore a path north from the Gulf Coast, many Americans remained without power, water and other basic necessities. The hurricane's destructive path necessitated a Red Cross response spanning 10 states, according to the non-profit.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KRON4

item.title: It's a 'very difficult time' for U.S. Jews as High Holy Days and Oct. 7 anniversary coincide
item.description: Known as “The Days of Awe,” Judaism’s High Holy Days — which begin on Wednesday — annually provide an emotional mix of celebration, introspection and atonement for Jews around the world.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A dockworkers strike could shut down Port Tampa Bay. Will it affect holiday shopping?
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. ports from Maine to Texas could shut down if a union representing about 45,000 dockworkers carries through with a threatened strike early Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden says he hopes to visit Helene-impacted areas this week if it doesn't impact emergency response
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden says he will visit Hurricane Helene-impacted areas this week as long as it does not disrupt rescue and recovery operations. Biden was briefed again on Sunday evening about the impact of the devastating storm on an enormous swath of the Southeast.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chastain passes Truex on late restart to win NASCAR Cup Series race at Kansas Speedway
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Kan. (AP) — Ross Chastain finally got to smash a watermelon for the first time this season Sunday at Kansas Speedway. Smashed the hopes of a bunch of playoff drivers trying to earn an automatic berth in the next round, too.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5
